title: caltha arctica subsp. caespitosa
intro: Caltha arctica subsp. caespitosa, also known as Arctic marsh-marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Caltha arctica subsp. caespitosa include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in folk medicine in Arctic regions.
